content: Now let me pull the full email content and check the production week attachment

cing.

---

## Zettelkasten Search Results

### No misspelling variants found
Keyword searches for `zettlekasten`, `zetle
kasten`, `zettle`, and `settle` returned **zero results** across
all content types. Every hit uses the correct spelling:

**Zettelkasten**.

---

### Hit 1 (×4

identical copies) — Fathom Meeting Recap

**Source:** Fathom <no-reply@fathom.video> —

*"Recap of your meeting with askeffi.ai"*
**Date:** January 29, 2026
**Email IDs:** `11a36f84`, `38211683`, `40581
fd4`, `65a26c84` (four copies of the same recap, sent

to different recipients; content is identical)
**Meeting duration:** 54

mins. Recording linked as a Fathom share.

---

**Verbatim — Key Takeaways section:**

> **"Mental Model" Strategy:** The team will build a "Zettelkasten"-style knowledge graph. This will move beyond simple RAG by

pre-processing data and tracking how ideas evolve over time, enabling queries like "How did our policy on X change?"

---

**Verbatim — "Mental Model" for Knowledge

section (full block):**

> **Problem:** The current RAG-only
approach is slow, expensive, and non-deterministic, providing incons

istent answers.
>
> **Solution: "Zettelkasten" Context Graph**
> - **Concept:** An emergent knowledge graph that connects ideas based on their content, not a pre-

defined structure.
> - **Process:**
>   1. Ingest data (e.g., email, transcript).
>   2. Extract its essence (summarize, tag).
>   3. Map it to the existing graph, connecting to related

nodes.
> - **Key Feature: Time-Awareness**
>   - The graph tracks how ideas and policies evolve over time.
>   - **Example Query:** "How did our policy on X change from Q3 to Q4?"
>

- **Value:** Enables proactive guidance by detecting shifts in team sentiment (e.g., from 80/20 confidence in option A to 51/49 against it) before a hard pivot is necessary.

---

**Note:** The "New Physics of Project Intelligence" attachment (Chris Baum, Jan 7, 2026) and several meeting transcripts discuss related concepts —

knowledge graphs, emergent memory, organizational context — but do not use the word Zettelkasten.

The term appears
**only once** in the project record: this Jan 29, 2026 internal
team meeting recap, as the named architecture for replacing the
RAG-only approach.
